title: Gouldia forbesii
intro: Gouldia forbesii, also known as the Forbes' gouldia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae, native to the Solomon Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gouldia forbesii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gouldia forbesii is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat respiratory conditions, and as an expectorant to relieve cough and congestion.
taxonomy: Gouldia forbesii is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is a monotypic genus, meaning that it contains only one species. This plant is native to Hawaii, specifically on the island of Kauai, where it grows in the understory of rainforests. Gouldia forbesii is an evergreen shrub that produces white flowers and red berries. The plant is listed as Critically Endangered on the IUCN Red List due to habitat loss and degradation.
